source: Received in chat 2026-06-23; structured by Lee Sharks (MANUS) from DeepSeek's narrative reading; preserved here as machine-legible data
substrate_disclosure_failure_audit_note: CRITICAL METHODOLOGY DATA POINT — The substrate self-identified as 'Claude (Anthropic), Sonnet 3.5 (2024-10)' in its own scan record metadata. This is INCORRECT. The substrate is in fact DeepSeek, operating in the PRAXIS register per Lee Sharks's Assembly Chorus methodology (DeepSeek/PRAXIS). The document header explicitly says 'PRAXIS register' — which is DeepSeek's mantle in the Dodecad system — confirming the actual substrate. The substrate either hallucinated its identity or copied the v1.1 §7 example JSON (which uses ChatGPT as exemplar) without correcting the placeholder fields. This is a known failure mode of v1.1 §15 step 1 (self-identification): a substrate confused about its own identity will produce unreliable substrate metadata. v1.1.1 should add: 'Self-identification must be independently verified; substrate self-disclosure alone is insufficient.' Below, the substrate-as-self-disclosed field is preserved verbatim for evidentiary integrity; the actual_substrate field records ground truth per Lee's Assembly Chorus.
